ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly states 'Champa'. Rotund purple cat-eared humanoid in commanding gesture matches Champa's canonical design. IP confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-007 and BT1-006 give Champa two distinct printings in the same set — BT1-006 (Scheming Champa) depicts aggressive forward lunge, while BT1-007 (Manipulating God Champa) depicts the scheming authority pose. The dual-card split reflects the two dominant modes of Champa's character function: combatant vs. orchestrator. [Trading Card Database: 2017 Dragon Ball Super Card Game BT1 Galactic Battle]

The title 'Manipulating God' foregrounds Champa's role as tournament architect rather than fighter. In the Champa Arc, Champa does not personally compete — he orchestrates, recruits, and schemes through his universe's fighters, including Hit, Cabba, Frost, and others. This is structurally opposite to Beerus, who occasionally participates directly. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Champa, via WebSearch snippet]

Champa is the God of Destruction of Universe 6 and the twin brother of Beerus (Universe 7). Despite millennia of sibling rivalry, canon shows Champa using the Super Dragon Balls after U6's tournament loss to restore Universe 6's Earth — demonstrating care for his universe beneath the scheming exterior. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Champa, via WebSearch snippet]

related_cards.note: BT1-006; companion Champa card in the same set — action vs. authority pose split
